description: Figure 19 presents experimental and simulated microstructure evolutions in low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) under constant and decreasing temperature deformation at 0.01 s⁻¹. Panel (a) compares reconstructed EBSD inverse pole figure maps and corresponding GOS (Grain Orientation Spread) maps at 0.9 true strain, contrasting a constant 950°C condition (grain size 52.01±3.48μm, DRX fraction 97.2%) against decreasing temperature paths from 1050°C to 950°C initiated at εI=0.2 (53.29±3.77μm, 96.1% DRX) and εI=0.4 (60.93±2.91μm, 97.2% DRX). Panel (b) shows reconstructed microstructures at intermediate strains (0.4-0.5), highlighting grain refinement differences between constant 1050°C (80.79±5.43μm), decreasing temperature (74.40±5.36μm), and constant 950°C (56.97±5.24μm). Panels (c) and (d) display 3D cellular automaton simulations of DRX progression: (c) at constant 950°C showing DRX fractions increasing from 76.9% to 99.5% with grain size reducing from 68.69μm to 52.52μm; (d) under decreasing temperature (1050°C→950°C) revealing multi-stage recrystallization (white matrix, green first-stage, red second-stage grains) with DRX fractions rising from 94.2% to 98.6% and final grain size 58.30μm. The figure validates that the inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D CA model accurately captures DRX kinetics and grain size evolution under varying thermal-mechanical conditions.
